claims.claim: Listing detail is the primary contact-host entry point and includes explicit no-booking/no-hold copy.
ContactHostButton starts or resumes a conversation and navigates to /messages/{conversationId} on success.
ContactHostButton can open a paywall dialog and post checkout offers to /api/payments/checkout.

startConversation enforces auth, rate limit, suspension, email verification, listing contactability, self-contact, host suspension, unit epoch, block, duplicate conversation, paywall, and contact-attempt behavior.
Message sending goes through sendMessage and sendConversationMessage with auth, rate, account, participant, listing, block, content flag, persistence, notification, and email paths.
GET and POST /api/messages are documented with auth, rate, access, polling, unread, mark-read, direct send, cache, and failure behavior.

The viewer-state API and public contact contract document contact CTA state, paywall summary, availability gates, suspended/blocked disabled reasons, private no-store behavior, and canBook/canHold false.
The data model and invariants cover conversation, message, notification, block, contact attempt, entitlement, consumption, listing, user, and physical unit state.

Auth/security docs distinguish protected and public/optional-auth surfaces, record source-observed permission gates, and include deterministic route-handler CSRF variant proof for the documented Contact Host mutation routes.
Interaction census rows include trigger, code path, state/API effect, UI result, failure behavior, evidence, and current evidence-backed test status.
State model and diagrams cover viewer state, contact button, paywall, conversation, messages, read state, typing, block, deletion, checkout return, and realtime/polling state.

The test matrix separates release-blocking checks from confidence-building checks, records current deterministic passes, the current checkout-route fixture-repair pass, current viewer-state route contract proof, deterministic per-route CSRF variant proof, historical and updated message-length boundary evidence, the current focused listing-detail Contact Host browser pass, the clean full Chromium messaging rerun, the mocked checkout-return browser pass, the scoped listing-detail state-matrix browser pass, the suspended/blocked source/test/fixture implementation and Chromium browser proof, focused WebKit/Mobile Chrome/Mobile Safari browser-matrix passes, the Firefox install plus post-install failure reclassification, historical Mobile Chrome evidence, and broader E2E/runtime gaps.
Runtime verification is explicitly marked PARTIALLY VERIFIED, with remaining unverified browser/runtime behavior not described as current verified behavior.

Message length is documented as an approved uniform 1000-character outbound contract across source and focused test sources, with focused Linux-side WSL Jest execution passing for the updated boundary tests.

claims.claim: The Phase 11 round-trip status reconciles historical Phase 1/2 and Phase 4 snapshots with current focused runtime/test evidence, records local Supabase Option A provider/RLS proof as locally verified, and keeps email, optional live-server parity, optional production/staging provider proof, and real payment-provider fulfillment as P2 or production-hardening items; it also records fallback/API/mocked-realtime reduction evidence, closes message-limit focused execution, adds the suspended/blocked contract/test-source reduction, closes suspended/blocked listing-detail Chromium execution in CH-E073, and adds schema/test-name detail for reconstruction.

The local Supabase Option A provider/RLS P1 is closed locally: fallback polling, API read/unread isolation, mocked client-side realtime insert handling, local-only RLS policy/publication audit, 28 direct RLS assertions, 34 local Realtime assertions, rollback, and cleanup are verified; production/staging RLS policies and production Prisma migration rollout are not claimed.

claims.claim: The focused Contact Host browser-matrix blocker is closed for the named Firefox focused-spec blockers: listing-detail runtime and messaging conversation specs pass on WebKit, Firefox, Mobile Chrome, and Mobile Safari after narrow test/setup fixes, and the practical combined two-spec Firefox run passes.

claims.claim: The approved suspended/blocked listing-detail proof path has explicit viewer-state disabled reasons, safe disabled CTA copy, dedicated seed/auth fixtures, passing focused route proof, focused Chromium E2E test source, and passing focused plus full listing-detail Contact Host Chromium browser proof.

The Contact Host email delivery P2 is reduced but real provider delivery remains unverified: mocked email invocation, recipient scoping, provider-client retry/circuit/no-key behavior, complete newMessage payload, preview normalization/truncation, fallback listing title, and template escaping are locally verified without provider calls.

The provider-level Supabase realtime/RLS proof path has local Option A evidence: local Supabase preflight/schema apply, local-only RLS/publication audit, direct RLS assertions, realtime delivery/non-delivery assertions, rollback, and cleanup passed. Option B isolated staging/provider verification remains optional production-hardening evidence, and production policy rollout requires separate approval.

coverageGaps.gap: Chromium dedupe passed earlier. Current verification passed focused listing-detail Contact Host runtime, deterministic API/action/component coverage, fallback polling, API read/unread isolation, mocked client-side realtime insert handling, local Supabase Option A provider/RLS proof, the full Chromium messaging journey after a narrow J25 test/setup synchronization fix, mocked Chromium checkout return / paid-unlock runtime, scoped listing-detail paywall-required/unavailable/migration-review/moderation-locked browser states, suspended/blocked listing-detail Chromium states, and focused WebKit/Firefox/Mobile Chrome/Mobile Safari listing-detail plus messaging specs. CH-E064 installed Firefox and reproduced focused Firefox image decode and NS_BINDING_ABORTED failures as test/setup evidence; CH-E065 closed those focused-spec blockers with narrow test/helper changes and a passing practical combined Firefox two-spec run. CH-E068 implements suspended/blocked listing-detail pre-click contract, fixture, route proof, and focused E2E test source; CH-E073 closes focused and full Chromium execution for those states. Remaining non-provider runtime/browser parity work is future confidence coverage. Optional production/staging Supabase provider proof and email remain separate P2 or production-hardening gaps.

Current focused deterministic tests passed for viewer-state route contract/listing-page, checkout creation route, checkout-session, CSRF/messages API, per-route CSRF variants, messaging/contact-host action/component coverage, affected contact/payment coverage, historical and updated message-length cap assertions, fallback polling, API read/unread isolation, and mocked client-side realtime insert handling. Focused listing-detail Contact Host E2E passed sequentially, the full Chromium messaging journey passed in CH-E057 after a test/setup synchronization fix, mocked checkout browser return passed in CH-E058, scoped listing-detail paywall/unavailable/migration/moderation browser states passed in CH-E059, and CH-E061 reran the focused state matrix plus full focused spec in WSL with `.last-run.json` passing. CH-E063 passed focused listing-detail and messaging specs on WebKit, Mobile Chrome, and Mobile Safari after narrow test/setup fixes. CH-E064 installed Firefox and reproduced the focused Firefox image decode and direct-navigation NS_BINDING_ABORTED failures; CH-E065 closed those blockers after narrow test/helper changes, with individual focused Firefox listing-detail and messaging specs plus the practical combined Firefox two-spec run passing. CH-E067 closed the message-length execution gate with a focused Linux-side WSL Jest pass. CH-E068 adds and passes suspended/blocked route coverage and adds browser test source; CH-E073 closes the focused suspended/blocked Chromium proof and full listing-detail Contact Host Chromium spec. CH-E071 closed deterministic per-route CSRF variant coverage for `/api/messages` and `/api/payments/checkout`. CH-E076 closes local Supabase Option A provider/RLS proof. Remaining broad browser/runtime parity items are P2 confidence gaps; production/staging Supabase provider proof remains optional P2 or production-hardening evidence.

Direct /api/messages route-handler status/cache-header evidence passed in CH-E055 for unread-count, thread, polling, conversation-list, send, and mark-read success paths after a test-only stale-fixture repair. Optional direct HTTP live-server status/cache/header capture remains confidence coverage if release acceptance requires transport-level proof outside route-handler Jest.

Focused route-handler tests passed for GET /api/listings/[id]/viewer-state status/cache headers, auth variants, privacy-safe fields, unavailable/moderation-locked states, paywall-required state, rate-limit passthrough, and fallback behavior. Optional direct HTTP live-server status/cache/header capture remains confidence coverage if release acceptance requires transport-level proof outside route-handler Jest.

Checkout creation route and checkout-session/status tests passed in CH-E053 after the checkout fixture freshness repair. Affected contact/payment coverage passed in CH-E054 after an adjacent phone-reveal fixture repair. Mocked Chromium checkout browser return and paid-unlock runtime passed in CH-E058. Real Stripe redirect and webhook/provider fulfillment remain unverified confidence coverage.

Fallback polling, API read/unread isolation, mocked client-side realtime insert handling, and local Supabase Option A provider/RLS proof are locally verified in CH-E062 and CH-E076. CH-E076 records local Supabase preflight/schema apply, local-only RLS/publication audit, 28 direct RLS assertions, 34 local Realtime assertions, rollback, and cleanup. This is local provider proof only; production/staging provider proof and production Prisma migration/RLS policy rollout remain optional production-hardening work requiring separate approval.

CH-E072 verifies safe local mocked behavior for Contact Host email invocation: internal notification plus mocked preference-aware email call are asserted for /api/messages, provider-client retry/circuit/no-key behavior is tested with mocked fetch, and no real email provider call was made. CH-E074 fixes and verifies the local newMessage payload/template contract with listingTitle, messagePreview, recipientName, senderName, conversationId, fallback title, normalized/truncated preview, and template escaping. Real provider acceptance, inbox delivery, bounce/webhook handling, and provider observability remain unverified.
